title: Creamy Irish Pork Knuckle Stew
description: A tangy and velvety Irish dish featuring pork knuckle, bhuna, and a keto-friendly twist, perfect for a hearty dinner on a cold monsoon night.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the pork knuckle with salt, black pepper, and caraway seeds.
In a large Dutch oven, sear the pork knuckle over medium-high heat until browned on all sides.
Remove the pork knuckle from the pot and set it aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for an additional minute.
Stir in the bhuna curry paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth to the pot and bring it to a boil.
Return the pork knuckle to the pot and cover it with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the pork knuckle for 2 hours.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the heavy cream.
Simmer the stew over low heat for an additional 10 minutes.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
